title: Cosmos Kaleido — mic-reactive k-fold kaleidoscope visualizer
summary: A mirrored k-fold kaleidoscope visualizer: spectrum bins scale and rotate the symmetric shapes while beats recolor the whole bloom. Reacts to the MICROPHONE live (gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable/denied) plus a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero-deps.
usage_notes: RUN: open and click "enable mic" to make the kaleidoscope dance to your voice/music; it falls back to a built-in beat if no mic is granted. KEY TECHNIQUES: an AnalyserNode feeds a 64-bin spectrum + level into render(spectrum, level, t); a seeded synthSpectrum(u) drives the same render for auto/fallback; alternating mirrored wedges build the k-fold symmetry; a low-band beat detector swaps the palette. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: FOLDS (symmetry count), PALS (color sets), LOOP (cycle length), and the beat threshold in detectBeat.
